summary: The `pnpm audit` command detected 1 moderate vulnerability in the `ip-address` package (XSS in Address6 HTML-emitting methods, GHSA-v2v4-37r5-5v8g), causing the audit step to exit with code 1 and fail the CI job.
root_cause: The dependency `ip-address` at version <=10.1.0 has a known XSS vulnerability (GHSA-v2v4-37r5-5v8g) in its Address6 HTML-emitting methods. It is pulled in transitively via `network__fetch > @pnpm/network.agent > @pnpm/network.proxy-agent > socks-proxy-agent > socks > ip-address`. The `pnpm audit` command found this vulnerability and exited with code 1, failing the CI job.

evidence.quote: │ moderate            │ ip-address has XSS in Address6 HTML-emitting methods   │
evidence.reason: Identifies the specific vulnerability detected by pnpm audit: a moderate-severity XSS issue in the ip-address package.
Shows the range of affected versions currently in use.
Indicates the fix requires upgrading ip-address to 10.1.1 or later.
Shows the transitive dependency chain through which ip-address is introduced.
Confirms that pnpm audit found exactly one vulnerability, triggering a non-zero exit.
Confirms the audit command exited with code 1, causing the CI job to fail.
Advisory reference for the ip-address XSS vulnerability.

suggested_fix: Update the `ip-address` transitive dependency to >=10.1.1 by upgrading the relevant packages in the chain (`socks`, `socks-proxy-agent`, or `@pnpm/network.proxy-agent`) so that `ip-address` resolves to a patched version, then re-run `pnpm audit` to confirm no vulnerabilities remain.
